question: How do multi-agent orchestration frameworks handle a worker pool where some workers have NO file/tool access and must receive fully self-contained context in their task payload? Specifically look at CrewAI, AutoGen/AG2, and LangGraph (or any other well-known framework you know of). For each: (1) does it have a documented pattern/term for a 'stateless' or 'context-complete' worker that can't read external files or call tools, only reason over what's inlined in its prompt? (2) how does the orchestrating layer decide what context to inline vs. what to leave for the worker to fetch itself? (3) is there a standard schema/shape for such a task payload?
context: Dark Factory (a personal multi-agent 'factory' project) has one main Claude Code agent with full file/tool access, and wants to occasionally offload narrow research questions to a friend-operated online agent that has NO file access at all and is less capable. The main agent must curate a fully self-contained packet (question + all needed context pasted as literal text) before handing work off, since the receiving agent can't go look anything up in a filesystem. Looking for prior art on this specific 'smart-agent-curates-for-dumb-agent' pattern, not general multi-agent orchestration.
note: NOT for engine/orchestrator.py auto-dispatch. External-research-agent consumer only -- see backlog/2026-07-04-research-agent-queue-handover.md
